metadata.source: Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, China Sporting Goods Federation, China Textile Industry Federation, National Bureau of Statistics

industry_overview.title: China Sportswear & Outdoor Industry Overview (2026)
industry_overview.summary: China is the world's largest producer of sportswear and outdoor equipment, supplying major global brands and rapidly growing domestic brands. The industry encompasses athletic apparel, footwear, outdoor gear (tents, backpacks, sleeping bags), and technical fabrics. In 2026, the industry is projected to exceed ¥1.2 trillion in revenue, driven by the fitness boom, outdoor recreation trends, winter sports legacy (post-2022 Olympics), and government support for national fitness initiatives.

key_policy_initiatives.name: National Fitness Program (2025-2030)
key_measures: Build 1,000 new sports parks and fitness trails
Subsidize sports participation and equipment purchases
Promote winter sports participation to 500 million people

key_policy_initiatives.name: Action Plan for High-Quality Development of Sporting Goods (2024-2026)
key_measures: Support R&D in functional fabrics and eco-friendly materials
Encourage digitalization and smart manufacturing in sportswear factories
Promote domestic brands in international markets
Establish national-level sporting goods innovation centers

current_status_2025.automation_rate: 55% (cutting and sewing lines in large factories)

upstream.description: Raw materials, functional fabrics, and components

subsectors: athletic apparel (jerseys, leggings, shorts, sports bras)
footwear (running shoes, hiking boots, training shoes)
outdoor gear (tents, backpacks, sleeping bags, camping furniture)
winter sports equipment (ski wear, snowboard wear, thermal layers)

applications: export to global markets (US, Europe, Japan, Korea, Southeast Asia)

key_hubs.supplier_example: Guangzhou Anta Supply Chain – sourcing and manufacturing base
Yue Yuen Industrial (Holdings) – world's largest athletic footwear manufacturer (Nike, Adidas etc.)
Ningbo Shenzhou Knitting – supplies Uniqlo, Nike, Adidas
Hangzhou Toread Outdoor Products – leading domestic outdoor brand
Jiangsu Easpring – down supplier for many brands
Anta, Xtep, 361°, Peak all headquartered here; thousands of suppliers
Xiamen Toread (outdoor brand) and many OEMs
Qingdao Zhongxin Outdoor Products – OEM for international brands

athletic_apparel.description: Jerseys, leggings, shorts, sports bras, training tops, yoga wear. Focus on moisture management, stretch, and comfort.
athletic_apparel.market_trends: Seamless knitting; recycled polyester; anti-odor treatments; compression wear.

outerwear.description: Softshell jackets, down coats, fleece jackets, rainwear, ski wear.
outerwear.market_trends: Waterproof/breathable membranes; eco-friendly DWR coatings; packable designs.

footwear.description: Running shoes, hiking boots, training shoes, basketball shoes, trail runners.
footwear.market_trends: Knitted uppers; bio-based foams; 3D printing for midsoles; sustainability (recycled materials).

outdoor_gear.description: Tents, backpacks, sleeping bags, camping furniture, hiking poles, headlamps.
outdoor_gear.market_trends: Ultralight tents; inflatable sleeping pads; solar-powered gear.

accessories.description: Hats, gloves, socks, buffs, gaiters, hydration packs.
accessories.market_trends: Merino wool socks; touchscreen-compatible gloves; UPF sun protection.

supplier_evaluation_metrics.target: ISO 9001, ISO 14001, Oeko-Tex 100, Bluesign (for fabrics), GOTS (organic)
Number of production lines, monthly output, specialized equipment (e.g., flatlock, bonding)
Ability to work with technical fabrics, seam sealing, lamination
Use of recycled materials, water treatment, energy efficiency

verification_methods: Conduct factory audit (working conditions, production quality)
Order samples for fit, performance, and durability testing
Use third-party inspection (SGS, Bureau Veritas) for pre-shipment
communication_best_practices: Provide detailed tech packs (flat sketches, measurements, materials, trim details)
Specify performance requirements (waterproof rating, breathability, tear strength)
Discuss sample development timeline and cost
Clarify labeling, packaging, and hanging requirements
Establish quality standards (AQL levels, acceptable defects)
sourcing_guide.payment_terms: Typical: 30% deposit, 70% balance before shipment (or against scanned B/L). For sample development, sample cost and courier charges may be prepaid.

faq.question: What are typical MOQs for sportswear in China?
faq.answer: For basic T-shirts, MOQ 500-1000 pieces per style/color. For technical outerwear, 300-600 pieces. For footwear, 1000-3000 pairs. Some factories accept lower MOQs (200-300) for sample orders at higher prices.

faq.question: Do Chinese suppliers provide custom branding and packaging?
faq.answer: Yes, most offer OEM/ODM services. They can sew your brand labels, hang tags, and provide custom poly bags or boxes. Usually there is a small additional cost.

faq.question: How can I ensure waterproof/breathable fabrics are authentic?
faq.answer: Request fabric specifications and test reports (water column, MVTR). Work with reputable suppliers. Some factories use branded membranes (Gore-Tex, Event) under license.

faq.question: What certifications are needed for outdoor gear in Europe/US?
faq.answer: For EU: CE, REACH, Oeko-Tex. For US: CPSIA for children's products, FTC compliance for labeling. Environmental claims need substantiation.

faq.question: Can I source sustainable sportswear in China?
faq.answer: Yes, many suppliers now offer recycled polyester (rPET), organic cotton, and eco-friendly dyes. Specify GRS (Global Recycled Standard) or GOTS certification.

glossary.AQL: Acceptable Quality Level – sampling standard for inspection.
glossary.BSCI: Business Social Compliance Initiative – social compliance audit.
glossary.DWR: Durable Water Repellent – coating for fabrics.
glossary.GOTS: Global Organic Textile Standard – organic fiber certification.
glossary.GRS: Global Recycled Standard – for recycled content.
glossary.MVTR: Moisture Vapor Transmission Rate – breathability measure.
glossary.Oeko-Tex: Certification for harmful substances in textiles.
glossary.Tech pack: Technical package with all product specifications.
glossary.TPU: Thermoplastic Polyurethane – used in shoe soles, laminates.
glossary.WRAP: Worldwide Responsible Accredited Production – social compliance.
